issue.title: Make check_upgrade_approval() actually validate the wasm_hash against grainlify-core governance proposals

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

issue.body:
<!-- ghit#filepath: /Users/jagadeesh/personal/projects/grantfox/.issues-work -->

## 📌 Description
`check_upgrade_approval(env, wasm_hash)` in `program-escrow/src/governance_integration.rs` (and the equivalent in `bounty_escrow/contracts/escrow/src/governance_integration.rs`) is a stub: it only delegates to `check_governance_version()` and never verifies that the specific `wasm_hash` was actually approved by an executed proposal. Meanwhile `grainlify-core/src/governance.rs` implements a full proposal/voting lifecycle (`create_proposal` storing `new_wasm_hash`, `cast_vote`, `finalize_proposal`, `execute_proposal`) with `ProposalStatus::Executed`. The integration link between the two is missing, so an admin could upgrade to a hash the DAO never approved.

> 💡 **Why it matters:** Treating "version is high enough" as "this upgrade is approved" defeats the purpose of governance and is a privilege-escalation risk for contract upgrades.

## 🧩 Requirements and context
- Define a cross-contract interface (Soroban `contractclient`) to query the governance contract for an executed proposal whose `new_wasm_hash` equals the requested hash.
- Update `check_upgrade_approval()` to return `true` only when the governance contract reports an `Executed` proposal matching the `wasm_hash` (and version check still passes).
- Add an explicit error/return path when no governance contract is configured (decide and document: deny vs admin-only fallback).
- Ensure `execution_delay` from `GovernanceConfig` is respected before approval is granted.
- Document the end-to-end upgrade-approval flow in `GOVERNANCE_INTEGRATION.md`.

**Non-functional requirements**
- Must be secure, tested, and documented.
- Should be efficient and easy to review.

## 🛠️ Suggested execution

**1. Fork the repo and create a branch**
```bash
git checkout -b feat/governance-upgrade-approval
```

**2. Implement changes**
- Write/modify the relevant source: `program-escrow/src/governance_integration.rs`, `bounty_escrow/contracts/escrow/src/governance_integration.rs`, `grainlify-core/src/governance.rs` (add a read-only approval query if absent)
- Write comprehensive tests: `program-escrow/src/test_governance_integration.rs` and `bounty_escrow/contracts/escrow/src/test_governance_integration.rs`
- Add documentation: `GOVERNANCE_INTEGRATION.md`
- Include `///` doc comments on the new approval-query function
- Validate security assumptions: unapproved hashes must be rejected even at a high version

**3. Test and commit**
- Run tests:
```bash
cargo test -p program-escrow test_governance_integration && cargo test -p escrow test_governance_integration
```
- Cover edge cases: matching-but-not-executed proposal, executed proposal with different hash, missing governance contract, execution delay not elapsed
- Include test output and security notes in the PR description.

**Example commit message**
```
feat(governance): validate upgrade wasm_hash against executed proposals
```

## ✅ Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `check_upgrade_approval` queries the governance contract for an executed matching-hash proposal
- [ ] Returns `false` when no matching executed proposal exists
- [ ] `execution_delay` is enforced before approval
- [ ] Behavior with no configured governance contract is explicit and documented
- [ ] Tests cover approved, unapproved, wrong-hash, and not-yet-executed cases

## 🔒 Security notes
This guards the upgrade authority; the cross-contract call must not be spoofable and must rely on the stored governance address. Reject upgrades by default when approval cannot be positively confirmed.

## 📋 Guidelines
- **Minimum 95% test coverage**
- **Clear documentation**
- **Timeframe: 96 hours**

issue.title: Add header-spoofing isolation tests for verifier endpoints in tests/multiVerifier.test.ts
issue.body:
<!-- ghit#filepath: /Users/jagadeesh/1nonly/grantfox/issues -->

## 📌 Description

The RBAC spec (`.kiro/specs/rbac-admin-verifier-tests/requirements.md`, Requirements 1 and 3) requires proving that the authorization middleware in `src/middleware/rbac.ts` reads role exclusively from the verified JWT and ignores spoofed headers such as `x-user-role: ADMIN` or `x-requested-role: SUPERADMIN`. Verifier endpoints (`POST /api/verifications` for VERIFIER+, `GET /api/verifications` for ADMIN only) need explicit bypass-prevention tests.

This issue adds the header-isolation and role-matrix tests for the verifier workflow.

## 🎯 Requirements and Context

- A valid USER JWT plus `x-user-role: ADMIN` header must still be denied 403 (Requirement 1.1).
- A VERIFIER JWT plus `x-requested-role: SUPERADMIN` must be processed as VERIFIER only (Requirement 1.2).
- Role headers without a valid JWT → 401 (Requirement 1.3).
- VERIFIER can POST `/api/verifications`; ADMIN inherits POST via hierarchy and is the only role allowed GET; USER is denied both (Requirement 3).
- Use `src/tests/fixtures/rbacArbitraries.ts` malicious-header generators.

## 🛠️ Suggested Execution

**1. Branch**

```bash
git checkout -b feature/verifier-header-isolation-tests
```

**2. Implement**

- Add header-isolation and verifier role-matrix cases to `tests/multiVerifier.test.ts` (or `src/tests/verifications.rbac.test.ts`, `*.test.ts`).
- Drive malicious header combinations from `rbacArbitraries.ts`.
- Document the bypass-prevention guarantees in `docs/SECURITY_INTEGRATION_TESTS.md`.

**3. Test, validate security, commit**

- The repo uses Bun — run `bun test tests/multiVerifier.test.ts` (or `npm test`).
- Validate security: no header combination escalates privilege beyond the JWT role.
- Edge cases: mixed-case header keys, multiple conflicting headers, missing JWT, expired JWT with spoofed header.

**Example commit message**

```
test(rbac): header-spoofing isolation coverage for verifier endpoints
```

## ✅ Guidelines

- Minimum 95% coverage of verifier RBAC branches.
- Clear documentation of bypass-prevention guarantees.
- No privilege escalation via headers.
- **Timeframe: 96 hours.**

## 🏷️ Labels

`type-testing` · `type-security` · `area-backend` · `MAYBE REWARDED` · `GRANTFOX OSS` · `OFFICIAL CAMPAIGN`

## 💬 Community & Support

- Join the Disciplr contributor Discord to coordinate and get unblocked fast: **https://discord.gg/xvNAvMJf**
- Please introduce yourself before starting so we can avoid duplicate work and pair you with a reviewer.

issue.title: Add property-based tests for redact() and maskIp() in privacy-logger middleware
issue.body:
<!-- ghit#filepath: /Users/jagadeesh/1nonly/grantfox/issues -->

## 📌 Description

The `privacy-logger` tasks file (`.kiro/specs/privacy-logger/tasks.md`, tasks 3.2–3.9) specifies eight property tests for the `redact()` utility and `maskIp()` that are not yet implemented. `src/tests/privacy-logger.test.ts` exists but lacks the fast-check property coverage that proves redaction holds at every depth and that masking is shape-correct for arbitrary IPs.

This issue adds those property-based tests so redaction invariants are exercised against generated inputs, not just hand-picked cases.

## 🎯 Requirements and Context

- P1: every key in `SENSITIVE_KEYS` is redacted at all nesting depths.
- P2: objects with only safe keys/values are returned deep-equal (non-sensitive fields preserved).
- P3: `redact` never mutates its input.
- P4/P5: arbitrary email-shaped and JWT-shaped string values are redacted regardless of key.
- P6: array elements are recursively redacted into a new array.
- P7: emitted log line has exactly the required key set, no extras.
- P8: IPv4 → `/^\d+\.\d+\.x\.x$/`; IPv6 → 3 real groups + 5 `xxxx` groups.
- Minimum 100 runs per property; tag each with its feature/property comment.

## 🛠️ Suggested Execution

**1. Branch**

```bash
git checkout -b feature/privacy-logger-property-tests
```

**2. Implement**

- Add the eight properties to `src/tests/privacy-logger.test.ts` (or a new `src/tests/privacy-logger.property.test.ts`, `*.test.ts`) using fast-check arbitraries.
- Reuse generators from `src/tests/fixtures/arbitraries.ts` where applicable.
- Add a short note in `docs/privacy-logging.md` describing the property invariants under test.

**3. Test, validate security, commit**

- The repo uses Bun — run `bun test src/tests/privacy-logger.property.test.ts` (or `npm test`).
- Validate security: any failing shrink must demonstrate a real redaction gap; fix the middleware if a property fails.
- Edge cases: circular references, mixed safe/sensitive keys, IPv6 shorthand, empty objects/arrays.

**Example commit message**

```
test(privacy): add fast-check property tests for redact and maskIp invariants
```

## ✅ Guidelines

- Minimum 95% test coverage on the redactor and masker.
- Clear documentation of the invariants asserted.
- Each property runs ≥ 100 generated cases.
- **Timeframe: 96 hours.**

## 🏷️ Labels

`type-testing` · `area-backend` · `MAYBE REWARDED` · `GRANTFOX OSS` · `OFFICIAL CAMPAIGN`

## 💬 Community & Support

- Join the Disciplr contributor Discord to coordinate and get unblocked fast: **https://discord.gg/xvNAvMJf**
- Please introduce yourself before starting so we can avoid duplicate work and pair you with a reviewer.

issue.title: fix(agent): count tokens, not just rows, as preflight compression progress

labels.name: user-88f76ac6/repo-cbe2230a
labels.description: Core agent runtime: loop, agent_init, prompt builder, context-compression, responses endpoint

issue.body:
## What does this PR do?

Preflight compression in ``agent/conversation_loop.py`` was treating *row-count reduction* as the only proxy for "did this compression pass make progress?". When a pass shrinks message contents (e.g., summarises tool outputs) without removing any rows, the loop misreads it as exhaustion and aborts with ``Context length exceeded`` — auto-resetting the gateway session even though the post-compression estimate is well below the model context window.

Fix: when re-estimating tokens after each pass, run the estimate *before* the progress check (instead of after), and treat either a row-count drop OR a token-count drop as progress.

## Related Issue

Fixes #39548

## Type of Change

- [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)

## Changes Made

- ``agent/conversation_loop.py``:
  - Add module-level helper ``_compression_made_progress(orig_len, new_len, orig_tokens, new_tokens) -> bool`` documenting the contract.
  - Capture ``_orig_tokens = _preflight_tokens`` at the top of each pass.
  - Move the post-pass ``estimate_request_tokens_rough`` call from *after* the bookkeeping (retry-counter resets, history clear) to immediately after ``_compress_context`` so the progress check has the fresh number to look at.
  - Replace ``if len(messages) >= _orig_len: break`` with ``if not _compression_made_progress(...): break``.
- ``tests/agent/test_compression_progress.py``: 7 unit tests pinning the helper's contract — row-only reduction, token-only reduction, both, neither, both growing, and the two cross-direction edges where one moves the right way and the other the wrong way.

## How to Test

```bash
pytest tests/agent/test_compression_progress.py -v
```

All 7 tests pass.

For the in-the-wild repro from #39548 — a 1M-context model with a long Telegram session that compressed from ~288k → ~183k tokens while message count stayed at 220 — preflight will now recognise the token reduction as progress and continue the conversation instead of auto-resetting the session.

## Notes on scope

There's a second, similar check at ``L3090`` (in-loop overflow recovery during execution). That site already has a ``new_ctx and new_ctx < old_ctx`` fallback alongside its row-count check, so it doesn't false-positive in the same way and is **intentionally left untouched** to keep this PR scoped to the preflight site that #39548 describes. Happy to extend if maintainers prefer one consistent helper across both sites.

## Checklist

### Code

- [x] I've read the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [x] Conventional commits format
- [x] Searched for existing PRs — none found at creation time
- [x] No ``simple_term_menu`` / no ``\033[K`` / no cross-toolset schema refs / no hardcoded ``~/.hermes`` / tool handlers still return JSON strings
- [x] **Does not break prompt caching**: compression is the one place context mutation is allowed by design; this PR only changes the success-criterion of an existing compression pass, not when or how compression runs

### Tests

- [x] 7 new unit tests on the extracted helper
- [x] Existing tests not affected (helper is additive; the loop change is semantically a strict expansion of the success criterion — anything that was previously progress still is)

## AI Disclosure

Diagnosed and implemented with AI assistance based on the reporter's detailed root-cause analysis and observed production logs.

comment.body: Bump on this one — still relevant and CLEAN on current main. It targets the #39548 P1: `_compression_made_progress` measured message *rows* rather than *tokens*, so a preflight pass that reclaimed tokens without dropping a row registered as no-progress and could spin. The fix switches the check to a token delta (+7 unit tests). Can rebase anytime if helpful.

issue.title: fix(openai): re-emit reasoning_content on outbound completions requests
issue.body:
## Summary

Fixes #10883.

The `@langchain/openai` Chat Completions converter already preserved `reasoning_content` from API responses into `additional_kwargs.reasoning_content` (see `converters/completions.ts:276-298` for response messages, and `:419-420` for streaming deltas). The outbound direction was missing: `convertMessagesToCompletionsMessageParams` and `convertStandardContentMessageToCompletionsMessage` dropped the field when converting an `AIMessage` back into a request payload.

OpenAI-compatible reasoning providers — DeepSeek being the headline case — require `reasoning_content` to be echoed back on the next turn after a tool call. Without the roundtrip, the second model call fails with:

```
BadRequestError: 400 The `reasoning_content` in the thinking mode must be passed back to the API.
```

`ChatDeepSeek` extends `ChatOpenAICompletions`, so it walks through the same converter path and benefits automatically. OpenAI's own endpoints do not populate `reasoning_content` on responses, so passthrough is naturally provider-scoped — no risk to vanilla OpenAI traffic.

## Changes

- `converters/completions.ts`: re-emit `reasoning_content` in both the v1/standard assistant branch and the legacy assistant branch of the outbound converter.
- `converters/tests/completions.test.ts`: three new unit tests — legacy path, v1 path, and a negative test asserting the field is absent when the source `AIMessage` has none.
- `.changeset/deepseek-reasoning-content-outbound.md`: patch bump for `@langchain/openai`.

## Test plan

- [x] `npx vitest run src/converters/tests/completions.test.ts` — 21/21 passing (3 new + 18 existing)
- [x] `npx prettier --check` clean on touched files
- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` introduces no new diagnostics on touched files (pre-existing baseline noise in other test files unchanged)
- [x] Reverse-direction test asserts that a stripped-fix copy of the converter would fail, confirming the test exercises the new code path

## AI Disclosure

This bug was identified and triaged with AI assistance; the fix and tests were drafted with AI assistance and reviewed manually. Path-sensitive reasoning (response_metadata.output_version="v1" routing through the standard converter vs. legacy) was verified against existing roundtrip tests before submission.

comment.body: Rebased this onto latest `main` — the converter was refactored since I opened this, so I re-applied the fix on top of the new `assistantParam`/tool-calls structure. CI is green and the unit test still covers the outbound `reasoning_content` round-trip (v1 + legacy paths). Mind taking a look when you have a moment @christian-bromann? It's a small one-function change in the Completions converter.
